p.strong: From Power-On Reset to 32-bit Protected Mode Kernel Entry
p.em: Technical Specification: Real Mode -> Protected Mode Transition

g.title: **CPU Initial State:** - EIP: 0xFFF0, CS: 0xF000 (Physical: 0xFFFF0) - Hardware discovery (PCI, USB) - Interrupt Vector Table (IVT) setup at 0x0000 - Searches CMOS/NVRAM for boot order
**BIOS INT 13h Operation:** - Loads first 512-byte sector to memory - Destination: 0x0000:0x7C00 - Validates signature 0x55, 0xAA at offset 510
**Environment: 16-bit Real Mode** - CS:IP = 0x0000:0x7C00 - DL = Boot Drive ID - Stack (SS:SP) must be manually configured - 1 MB addressable limit (20-bit)
**Memory Access Above 1MB:** 1. BIOS INT 15h, ax=2401h 2. Keyboard Controller (Port 0x64/0x60) 3. Fast A20 (Port 0x92 bit 1)
**Global Descriptor Table:** - Define Null, Code, Data segments - Base: 0x0, Limit: 0xFFFFF, G=1 (4KB granularity) - Load GDTR with pointer to descriptor
**Protection Enable Bit:** - cli (Clear Interrupts) - mov eax, cr0; or eax, 1; mov cr0, eax - **CRITICAL:** CPU is in 32-bit mode, but CS still points to 16-bit segment
**Instruction Synchronization:** - jmp 0x08:PM_ENTRY - 0x08 = Selector for Code Segment in GDT - Flushes instruction prefetch/pipeline - Serializes CPU state
**Hardware Context:** - 32-bit registers (EAX, ESP, etc.) - Segment Registers (DS, ES, SS) = 0x10 (Data Selector) - Full 4GB address space access
**High-Level Language Entry:** - Stack pointer (ESP) pointing to safe RAM - GDT/IDT finalized - C/C++ global constructors called (if applicable)
**Fatal Exception Chain:** - Double fault fails to be handled - CPU enters shutdown cycle - Result: Instant hardware reboot
Memory wraparound test failed. Cannot address > 1MB.
BIOS INT 13h returned CF=1 or invalid MBR signature.
**CPU Initial State:** - EIP: 0xFFF0, CS: 0xF000 (Physical: 0xFFFF0) - Hardware discovery (PCI, USB) - Interrupt Vector Table (IVT) setup at 0x0000 - Searches CMOS/NVRAM for boot order
**BIOS INT 13h Operation:** - Loads first 512-byte sector to memory - Destination: 0x0000:0x7C00 - Validates signature 0x55, 0xAA at offset 510
**Environment: 16-bit Real Mode** - CS:IP = 0x0000:0x7C00 - DL = Boot Drive ID - Stack (SS:SP) must be manually configured - 1 MB addressable limit (20-bit)
**Memory Access Above 1MB:** 1. BIOS INT 15h, ax=2401h 2. Keyboard Controller (Port 0x64/0x60) 3. Fast A20 (Port 0x92 bit 1)
**Global Descriptor Table:** - Define Null, Code, Data segments - Base: 0x0, Limit: 0xFFFFF, G=1 (4KB granularity) - Load GDTR with pointer to descriptor
**Protection Enable Bit:** - cli (Clear Interrupts) - mov eax, cr0; or eax, 1; mov cr0, eax - **CRITICAL:** CPU is in 32-bit mode, but CS still points to 16-bit segment
**Instruction Synchronization:** - jmp 0x08:PM_ENTRY - 0x08 = Selector for Code Segment in GDT - Flushes instruction prefetch/pipeline - Serializes CPU state
**Hardware Context:** - 32-bit registers (EAX, ESP, etc.) - Segment Registers (DS, ES, SS) = 0x10 (Data Selector) - Full 4GB address space access
**High-Level Language Entry:** - Stack pointer (ESP) pointing to safe RAM - GDT/IDT finalized - C/C++ global constructors called (if applicable)
**Fatal Exception Chain:** - Double fault fails to be handled - CPU enters shutdown cycle - Result: Instant hardware reboot
Memory wraparound test failed. Cannot address > 1MB.
BIOS INT 13h returned CF=1 or invalid MBR signature.
